zotero.abstractNote: Active exploration is a fundamental challenge in reinforcement learning and control, where an agent must interact with an unknown environment to learn its dynamics efficiently. While many existing methods rely on uncertainty-based exploration, they often struggle with the exploration-exploitation trade-off in complex, high-dimensional dynamical systems. In this work, we propose a novel framework for optimistic active exploration of dynamical systems. Our approach leverages the principle of optimism in the face of uncertainty by constructing a set of plausible dynamics models and selecting actions that maximize the potential for information gain or task performance. We demonstrate the effectiveness of our method on a range of challenging control tasks, showing that it outperforms existing state-of-the-art exploration strategies in terms of sample efficiency and final performance.
zotero.conferenceName: Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems
zotero.proceedingsTitle: Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems 36 (NeurIPS 2023)
